title: Design full browser QA orchestrator
description: Design and plan the ambitious Surface browser QA orchestrator direction with agent-browser as the primary live-browser engine. This covers public commands, MCP tools, QA run state, evidence artifacts, exploration strategy, security/privacy controls, test strategy, and phased implementation work before code changes.
design: Hybrid browser QA orchestrator: scripted task-flow execution is the deterministic core for replay, validation, and CI; autonomous exploration is a discovery layer that finds states/issues and emits replayable candidate flows/findings. surface qa orchestrates both, with flow artifacts reusable through CLI/MCP and evidence persisted only when needed for findings, replay, validation, or reports.

title: REL-013 Fix release workflow tarball publish paths
description:
Summary: The manual first publish revealed that pnpm --dir packages/... pack --pack-destination .release-packs writes tarballs under each package directory, but .github/workflows/release.yml publishes root-relative .release-packs/*.tgz paths.

Why this matters: The GitHub release workflow would fail during npm publish even after trusted publishing is configured.

Expected outcome: Update the release workflow to pack into a root-level absolute or correctly referenced directory, or publish from the actual generated package-local tarball paths.

Validation: A dry-run or local reproduction proves the workflow publish paths match the tarball paths before initial release.
close_reason: Fixed release workflow to pack npm tarballs into a root-level PACK_DIR and added release metadata regression coverage; validation passed: focused release metadata test, root pack path reproduction, pnpm run release:verify, pnpm run check.

title: REL-012 Define low/zero GitHub Actions usage policy
description:
Summary: Decide and implement the project's policy for minimizing or eliminating GitHub Actions minutes before initial release.

Context: Current ci.yml runs the full local quality gate on every pull_request to main and every push to main. GitHub's current billing docs state standard GitHub-hosted runners are free for public repositories, and self-hosted runners are free, but private repositories consume included/billable minutes on GitHub-hosted runners. npm trusted publishing/provenance creates a tension: it is most secure from GitHub Actions/OIDC, so eliminating Actions entirely may require giving up trusted publishing or using a manual/token-based publish fallback.

Brainstormed options:
1. Local-first/manual CI: make GitHub CI workflow_dispatch only or manually triggered for release candidates; rely on local pnpm run check, pack smoke tests, and scaffold/Beads gates for normal development. Lowest GitHub minutes, weakest automatic PR signal.
2. Conditional low-minute CI: keep PR checks but add concurrency cancel-in-progress, remove redundant push-to-main runs, use paths/paths-ignore carefully, split cheap smoke checks from expensive browser/e2e/release checks, and avoid macOS/Windows/larger runners. Balanced default.
3. Self-hosted runner CI: move routine checks to a trusted self-hosted runner while keeping GitHub-hosted release workflow only if npm trusted publishing/provenance is required. Near-zero GitHub-hosted minutes, but adds runner maintenance and security constraints, especially for untrusted PRs.

Scope: Choose the policy, update or create workflow tasks accordingly, document minute-budget rules, ensure skipped workflows do not leave required PR checks pending, and define when GitHub-hosted Actions are allowed (for example only manual release publishing with npm trusted publishing).

Acceptance: The release plan includes an explicit GitHub Actions minute policy, allowed triggers, runner strategy, branch-protection implications, and a target of either zero routine GitHub-hosted CI minutes or a bounded one-run-per-release workflow.
close_reason: Defined local-first low/zero GitHub Actions minute policy and updated CI/release workflow constraints accordingly.

Acceptance: A release check command proves npm tarballs install from a clean temp directory and the CLI runs from the packed artifact.

title: REL-010 Perform initial release dry run and tag readiness verification
description:
Summary: Run the final pre-tag release verification before publishing.

Context: The release should not be tagged until the repository, package artifacts, npm publishing setup, GitHub release workflow, and Homebrew formula all pass their dry-run gates.

Scope: Fetch latest main, verify clean status and target SHA, run pnpm run check, run npm package dry-runs and packed install smoke tests, run release workflow dry-run where possible, run Homebrew formula audit/test, and prepare the semver tag/release notes.

Acceptance: A concrete go/no-go report identifies the exact tag, commit SHA, package names, artifact checks, and Homebrew formula checks before the initial GitHub release is created.
notes:
Cost-control constraint: final release dry run must report expected GitHub Actions minute usage and confirm the chosen policy from REL-012. If zero GitHub-hosted minutes is selected, verify release can be completed without GitHub Actions or document the accepted exception for npm trusted publishing.

Remaining go/no-go blockers: npm trusted publisher setup (surface-h1t) and Homebrew published-tarball checksum/tap audit (surface-0wo).
close_reason: Release readiness verified for v0.1.1: pnpm run check passed, pnpm run release:verify passed, all eight npm packages visible at 0.1.1, clean npm install reports 0.1.1, Homebrew formula install/test/audit passed, no v* tags or GitHub releases exist yet, release workflow has zero runs.

title: REL-001 Resolve release branch state and dirty worktree
description:
Summary: Prepare the repository state before any release tag is created.

Context: Current worktree showed modified .scaffold/state.json and untracked docs/reviews/post-implementation-review.md, and local origin/main appeared behind the previously merged PR #71 state.

Scope: Fetch latest origin/main, verify the intended release commit, resolve or intentionally include dirty files, and document the exact commit SHA that should be tagged.

Acceptance: git status is clean on the release branch, origin/main is current, and the release commit is explicitly identified.
close_reason: Fetched and rebased onto origin/main e2b0fde; resolved prior dirty worktree by including release-prep files in commit 28d0a17. Working tree is clean and branch is ahead of origin/main by one commit.

title: REL-004 Add release documentation and legal files
description:
Summary: Add the user-facing and legal files expected for an initial public release.

Context: No root README.md, LICENSE, CHANGELOG.md, or SECURITY.md was visible in the release-readiness pass, and npm dry-run tarballs did not include package docs or license files.

Scope: Add root README with install/quickstart examples, LICENSE matching package metadata, CHANGELOG/release notes for the initial release, SECURITY.md, and package-level docs as needed for npm tarballs.

Acceptance: GitHub and npm package pages have useful install/usage docs, license is present in release artifacts, and initial release notes are ready.
close_reason: Added README, LICENSE, CHANGELOG, SECURITY, NOTICE, release docs, and package-level README/LICENSE files for npm artifacts.

title: REL-006 Add GitHub release workflow for npm publishing
description:
Summary: Add release automation for GitHub Releases and npm publication.

Context: The repo currently has CI but no release workflow. npm trusted publishing/provenance requires GitHub-hosted runners, id-token: write, and package repository.url matching the GitHub repo.

Scope: Add a release workflow that runs full checks, builds packages, runs pack/install smoke validation, publishes selected packages to npm with public access/provenance or trusted publishing, and creates/updates the GitHub Release from a semver tag.

Acceptance: Release workflow is present, least-privilege permissions are set, release execution is documented, and dry-run paths are available before publishing.
notes: Cost-control constraint: release automation should follow REL-012. Prefer manual/tag-only release triggers, concurrency cancel-in-progress, Linux standard runners only, no scheduled runs, no OS matrix, short artifact retention, and no GitHub-hosted workflow for routine development if the selected policy is zero routine minutes. If npm trusted publishing/provenance is required, bound GitHub-hosted usage to the minimum release publish job.
close_reason: Added manual-only .github/workflows/release.yml with local gates, release artifact validation, OIDC/provenance configuration, ordered npm publish, GitHub release creation, concurrency, and bounded runner usage.

title: REL-003 Prepare publishable package manifests
description:
Summary: Update package manifests so selected packages can be published to npm.

Context: packages/cli, packages/core, packages/mcp, and packages/grounding are private and versioned 0.0.0. npm refuses packages with private:true.

Scope: Remove private:true from publishable packages, set real versions, add description/repository/license/homepage/bugs/keywords/engines metadata, set publishConfig.access=public for scoped public packages, and keep non-published packages intentionally private.

Acceptance: npm manifest metadata is complete for each publishable package and npm publish would not be blocked by private:true or missing selected names.
close_reason: Prepared publishable package manifests for @zigrivers public package names with version 0.1.0, MIT license, repository metadata, public provenance publishConfig, files, and workspace dependency renames.

title: PIR-007 Replace seeded-fixture CLI e2e with real audit pipeline coverage
description:
Summary: CLI smoke and SC-6 benchmark tests exercise the hardcoded seeded-fixture path rather than real grounding/lens evaluation through the CLI.

Severity: P1
Sources: Claude post-implementation review; local verification.
Locations:
- tests/e2e/cli-smoke.e2e.test.ts:62
- tests/benchmark/sc6-benchmark.test.ts

Why this matters: These tests give false confidence that the closed loop works end-to-end. They can pass while the CLI never runs actual grounding tools, lenses, or scoring.

Expected outcome: After PIR-001, update e2e/benchmark fixtures so findings come from real measured grounding/lens execution. Keep seeded defects, but assert the production audit pipeline detects and resolves them.

Validation: `pnpm run test:e2e`, `pnpm run benchmark:sc6`, and `pnpm run check` pass with no production seeded-fixture shortcut.
notes: Claim released: this is not satisfied by removing the seeded shortcut. It needs a real measured CLI grounding path for seeded fixtures (for example wiring default accessibility grounding for captured HTML/URL targets), then e2e/benchmark should assert detection and resolution through the shared audit service.
close_reason: Implemented real measured CLI contrast grounding for seeded fixtures; validation passed: pnpm run test:e2e, pnpm run benchmark:sc6, pnpm run check.

title: PIR-006 Validate all durable state fields with canonical schemas
description:
Summary: ProjectStateSnapshotSchema validates only a subset of durable state fields and passthroughs findings, backlog, runRecords, and verdicts without canonical zod validation.

Severity: P1
Sources: Codex + Claude post-implementation review.
Location: packages/core/src/state-store.ts:38

Why this matters: ADR-005 requires trust invariants at boundaries. Unvalidated durable state lets malformed findings/backlog/verdicts persist and later drive gates, exports, or reports.

Expected outcome: Extend persisted state validation to all durable fields using canonical schemas, including findings, backlog, runRecords, verdicts, baselines, and tracked findings. Add migrations for legacy loose state instead of silently preserving invalid passthrough data.

Validation: Add corrupt/invalid state tests for each durable field and confirm StateStore reports state_corrupt or migrates intentionally.
close_reason: Validated all durable state fields with canonical schemas: findings, backlog, runRecords, verdicts, baselines, and trackedFindings. Added corrupt read/write tests and updated CLI verdict/state fixtures to canonical records. pnpm run check passed.

title: PIR-005 Redact agent-browser command and stderr details in errors
description:
Summary: agent-browser capture errors expose raw commandArgs and stderr in SurfaceError details, which can then flow into CLI/MCP error envelopes.

Severity: P1
Sources: Codex post-implementation review.
Location: packages/core/src/capture.ts:1864

Why this matters: commandArgs can contain auth-state paths or target details, and stderr from an external capture tool may include sensitive target/captured-content information. This conflicts with the privacy-safe logging and data-boundary requirements.

Expected outcome: Omit or redact auth-state paths, command arguments, and raw stderr from user-facing error details. Keep safe metadata such as backend id, normalized failure reason, exit code, and a bounded redacted stderr summary if needed.

Validation: Add tests for agent-browser command failures that assert error envelopes do not include raw auth-state paths, command arrays, secrets, or captured content.
close_reason: Redacted agent-browser command failure details to omit command args and raw stderr while preserving safe metadata; validation passed: pnpm run check.

title: PIR-004 Make pipeline state transitions atomic for overlapping runs
description:
Summary: Pipeline orchestration uses separate readState/writeState cycles around stage transitions, so overlapping runs can interleave and overwrite state despite individual StateStore operation locks.

Severity: P1
Sources: Codex post-implementation review; local verification.
Locations:
- packages/core/src/pipeline-orchestrator.ts around stage metadata read/write transitions
- tests/acceptance/epic-e5-closed-loop.test.ts:273 overlapping-runs acceptance coverage is skipped

Why this matters: US-041 requires concurrency-safe, resumable state. Per-operation locks do not protect multi-step read-modify-write orchestration.

Expected outcome: Use StateStore.updateState or an equivalent transactional API for pipeline stage transitions, with conflict handling for concurrent run metadata.

Validation: Unskip/implement the overlapping-runs integration test against the real FileStateStore and confirm neither run corrupts or loses state.
close_reason: Pipeline stage transitions now use StateStore.updateState for atomic read-modify-write; overlapping-run acceptance coverage unskipped; validation passed: focused orchestrator/acceptance tests and pnpm run check.

title: PIR-003 Move capture and context artifact writes behind StateStore
description:
Summary: Capture backends and context ingestion write files directly under `.surface/` using filesystem writes, bypassing the StateStore sole-writer/locking/atomic persistence boundary.

Severity: P1
Sources: Codex + Claude post-implementation review; local verification.
Locations:
- packages/core/src/capture.ts:317 and related backend artifact writes
- packages/core/src/context-ingestor.ts materializes `.surface/context-inputs/*` directly

Why this matters: ADR-003 and the implementation plan state that StateStore is the sole `.surface/` writer. Direct writes bypass locking and atomicity, creating split-write and data-retention risk.

Expected outcome: Route capture/context artifacts through StateStore.writeArtifact or an injected artifact persistence interface with the same lock and atomic-write policy. If any directory is intentionally outside StateStore, document the explicit ADR exception.

Validation: Add tests proving capture and context materialization use the StateStore-backed artifact writer and preserve redaction/sha256 metadata.
close_reason: Completed StateStore-backed capture and context artifact persistence

title: PIR-002 Persist MCP runs and closed-loop state via shared StateStore
description:
Summary: The MCP server keeps runs, findings, baselines, verdicts, and tracked findings in process-local session maps instead of persisting through the shared `.surface` StateStore.

Severity: P1
Sources: Codex post-implementation review; local verification.
Location: packages/mcp/src/index.ts around session-state handling and tool calls.

Why this matters: MCP and CLI can disagree about project state, and MCP audit history is lost on restart. That violates ADR-003 and ADR-008 expectations that interface adapters share durable local project state.

Expected outcome: Back MCP tools with the same StateStore and shared audit/closed-loop services used by CLI. Persist runs, findings, backlog, baselines, verdicts, and tracked findings under `.surface` so CLI and MCP observe the same state.

Validation: Add an integration test that runs an MCP audit, restarts/recreates the server, then reads the same run through MCP and CLI-visible state.
close_reason: Completed MCP StateStore persistence and restart hydration

title: PIR-001 Route CLI audit through shared evaluation pipeline
description:
Summary: The CLI audit command bypasses the real Surface evaluation path and synthesizes seeded-fixture findings instead of running capture, grounding tools, lenses, scoring, backlog synthesis, and tracking through shared core services.

Severity: P1
Sources: Codex + Claude post-implementation review; local verification.
Locations:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts:840 auditTarget calls findingsForSeededFixture(target.value)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts:1804 findingsForSeededFixture hardcodes a seeded low-contrast finding
- packages/mcp/src/index.ts:514/541/551 shows MCP has a real grounding/lens path via selectLensExecutionPlan and findingsForPlan

Why this matters: The primary CLI surface reports mock/prototype findings and diverges from ADR-002, ADR-005, ADR-012, and the release-gate user stories. It can pass seeded smoke tests without exercising the product's actual value path.

Expected outcome: Introduce or reuse a shared core audit/application service that both CLI and MCP call. CLI should parse args, call the shared service, persist validated state, and render output. Seeded-fixture detection should live in tests/fixtures or an explicit test backend, not shipped production CLI logic.

Validation: Add/replace CLI e2e coverage so `surface audit` produces findings through real grounding/lens execution, then run pnpm run check.
close_reason: Implemented shared core audit service used by CLI and MCP; removed production seeded-fixture CLI audit path; updated CLI/MCP/e2e/benchmark coverage; pnpm run check passed. Grok review attempted three times but failed before returning findings (max turns reached).

title: REL-014 Fix CLI version output for published package
description:
Summary: The Homebrew-installed @zigrivers/surface@0.1.0 CLI runs, but `surface --version` prints `0.0.0` instead of the published package version `0.1.0`.

Why this matters: Release validation and user support expect the installed CLI to report the same semver version as npm/Homebrew.

Expected outcome: Wire the CLI version source to package metadata or generated build metadata so npm and Homebrew installs report `0.1.0` for this release line and future versions.

Validation: `surface --version` from a clean npm install and Homebrew install reports the package version.
close_reason: Fixed CLI version output to read package metadata; verified clean npm install and Homebrew install both report 0.1.1.

title: REL-008 Define Homebrew tap and formula strategy
description:

Context: Homebrew core expects stable tagged releases and notability/review requirements, so a custom tap is the practical initial-release path.

Scope: Choose tap repository/name, decide whether formula installs from GitHub source tarball, npm package, or bundled release artifact, define Node dependency requirements, and document brew install commands.

Acceptance: Homebrew distribution strategy is documented and the target tap repository/package path is known.
close_reason: Documented custom Homebrew tap strategy in docs/release/homebrew.md and added packaging/homebrew/surface.rb formula template for zigrivers/surface.

title: REL-009 Add Homebrew formula and audit validation
description:
Summary: Create and validate a Homebrew formula for the initial release path.

Context: Homebrew formulae need a stable URL/checksum, homepage, license, dependencies, install block, and test block; formulae should pass brew audit and brew test.

Scope: Add formula in the selected tap, use the release tarball or selected artifact URL, include checksum/livecheck where appropriate, install the surface CLI, and add a test that verifies the installed command.

Acceptance: brew install, brew test, and brew audit --new --formula pass for the formula.

SHA256 for Homebrew formula: 105a3c8f98a1ee2a0bc194b7cb01e0a6ed0454c7500423b169c0d7591d7867ec. Remaining work: replace the placeholder checksum in the Homebrew formula/tap, then run brew install/test/audit validation.
close_reason: Completed Homebrew tap and formula validation. Created public tap zigrivers/homebrew-surface, added Formula/surface.rb with npm tarball URL and SHA256, fixed npm same-day install and libexec install layout, pushed tap, validated: ruby -c, brew tap, brew install --build-from-source zigrivers/surface/surface, brew test zigrivers/surface/surface, brew audit --new --formula zigrivers/surface/surface.

title: PIR-011 Remove committed sensitive .surface context artifacts
description:
Summary: Materialized context-input artifacts are committed under packages/core/.surface/context-inputs, even though captured DOM/source/CSS evidence should be local/ephemeral and git-ignored.

Severity: P2
Sources: Claude post-implementation review; local file tree verification.
Location: packages/core/.surface/context-inputs/*

Why this matters: Context/capture artifacts may contain PII, proprietary source, DOM, CSS, or screenshots. Keeping these in version control contradicts ADR-003/ADR-013 retention/data-boundary policy.

Expected outcome: Add appropriate ignore rules for `.surface/context-inputs/` and `.surface/captures/`, remove committed generated artifacts, and retain only intentional durable state/config artifacts.

Validation: git status and a fixture test/build run should not recreate tracked `.surface/context-inputs` files.
close_reason: Already satisfied: no tracked .surface artifacts; ignore rule covers package-local context/capture artifacts; context-ingestor tests pass

title: PIR-010 Reconcile SurfaceError taxonomy with ADR-014
description:
Summary: The implemented SurfaceError taxonomy diverges from ADR-014 by omitting documented GroundingError/AdapterError categories and adding RuntimeError.

Severity: P2
Sources: Claude post-implementation review.
Location: packages/core/src/errors.ts

Why this matters: Grounding and adapter failures need stable typed homes for CLI/MCP mapping, reporting, and actionable errors. Drift between ADR and code weakens the error contract.

Expected outcome: Either update the implementation to match ADR-014 or amend the ADR with the intentional taxonomy change. Keep error codes mapped to documented kinds.

Validation: Add tests for grounding and adapter failure mapping through core, CLI, and MCP envelopes.
close_reason: Completed SurfaceError taxonomy reconciliation with GroundingError/AdapterError and ADR/API catalog updates

title: PIR-009 Implement or remove ignored CLI flags
description:
Summary: Several CLI flags are accepted but have no effect, including validate --run, gate --policy, and parts of gate/status behavior.

Severity: P2
Sources: Claude post-implementation review.
Locations:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts around validateRun/evaluateGate/readStatus

Why this matters: The CLI is scriptable/agent-facing. Accepting flags that silently do nothing violates NFR-CLI-1 expectations and can mislead CI or agents.

Expected outcome: Implement each accepted flag's behavior or remove the flag. Add CLI contract tests that prove each remaining flag changes behavior as documented.

Validation: Targeted CLI tests plus pnpm run check.
close_reason: Implemented validate --run and gate --policy flag behavior with CLI contract tests

title: PIR-008 Implement or remove advertised unimplemented MCP tools
description:
Summary: MCP advertises tools such as surface_alternatives, surface_run, and surface_next while handlers return not-implemented/unknown-step behavior.

Severity: P2
Sources: Claude post-implementation review.
Location: packages/mcp/src/index.ts around TOOL_ORDER and unimplemented tool handlers.

Why this matters: Versioned MCP schemas should not advertise capabilities that always fail. This violates the agent-facing contract and creates integration confusion.

Expected outcome: Either implement the advertised tools or remove them from the public registry/schema until implemented. File separate Beads for any deferred tool work.

Validation: MCP registry snapshot and tool-call tests prove every advertised tool has working behavior or is absent from the schema.
close_reason: Completed: removed deferred MCP tools from the advertised registry/schema, updated contract docs/tests, and filed follow-up issues for implementing each deferred tool.
